---
title: "stat-perf"
format: html
---

Sensitivity = $\mathbb{P}(+|D)$

1 - Sensitivity = $1 - \mathbb{P}(+|D) = \mathbb{P}(-|D)$

Specificity = $\mathbb{P}(-|ND)$

1 - Specificity = $1 - \mathbb{P}(-|ND) = \mathbb{P}(+|ND)$

Positive likelihood ratio tells you how many times more likely a positive test result is to come from someone with disease than from someone without disease.

$$LR^+ = \frac{\mathbb{P}(+|D)}{\mathbb{P}(+|ND)} = \frac{Sens}{1 - Spec}$$

Likewise, negative likelihood ratio is how many times more likely a **negative** test result is to come from someone with disease than from someone without disease.

$$LR^+ = \frac{\mathbb{P}(-|D)}{\mathbb{P}(-|ND)} = \frac{1 - Sens}{Spec}$$
